observations.summary: Three halts fired across validate-story-2, implement-story, and e2e-tests — all with outcomeHistory ["COMPLETE"] and no question in any responseExcerpt; the validate-story-2 halt had a 7-hour duration (8x the ~53min average), strongly suggesting an infra stall misclassified as a halt rather than a genuine decision escalation.
observations.evidence: Journal step_end for validate-story-2 (durationMs: 25488607, halts: 1, outcomeHistory: ["COMPLETE"], responseExcerpt is a normal validation summary about Vercel API v10→v11 changes with no question). Journal step_end for implement-story (halts: 1, outcomeHistory: ["COMPLETE"], responseExcerpt is an NFR pattern verification table with no question). Journal step_end for e2e-tests (halts: 1, outcomeHistory: ["COMPLETE"], responseExcerpt is a test summary update with no question). No runner-errors.jsonl entries for this run. No trace directory exists at _bmad-output/pipeline/traces/4.1-a1-20260711T211933/ — trace-view reports 'no trace for this step' for all steps, preventing mid-stream inspection of what triggered the halt form. Trends show haltsPerStory: 3 for 4.1 (first story with halts > 0).
observations.hypothesis: The halts are machinery artifacts (runner stalls, classifier ambiguity, or provider API interruptions) rather than genuine decision escalations — no responseExcerpt contains a question, and the 7-hour validate-story-2 duration is inconsistent with a question-answer cycle. The outcomeHistory pattern (["COMPLETE"] with halts > 0) differs from the standard phantom-halt pattern (which expects QUESTION in outcomeHistory), suggesting the halt form fired mid-stream but the agent eventually produced a COMPLETE outcome.
observations.nextStep: If halts recur on the next story with outcomeHistory ["COMPLETE"] and no question in the responseExcerpt, check the opencode runner logs for stalls, classifier issues, or provider API interruptions during the halted steps. Enabling trace recording for the next run would allow widening into the mid-stream conversation to confirm whether a QUESTION was posed and resolved, or whether the halt fired without any question.

observations.summary: The 3.12 update_step to implement-story is working — Story 4.1's own test file had no stale headers (header correctly says 'Tests are active (green phase)'); the review-tests guard fixed 4 stale markers from earlier stories (1.3, 3.2), continuing to clean up historical debt rather than indicating new recurrence.
observations.evidence: Journal step_end for review-tests: 'All 8 ATDD tests are active (green phase) with real assertions. Header correctly says "Tests are active (green phase)." No skipped tests, no empty stubs, no stale markers.' for Story 4.1's test file (vercel-config.spec.ts:7-8 confirmed by reading the actual file). 4 stale markers from Stories 1.3 and 3.2 were fixed (test-review-validation-report-4-1.md:82-149 — repo-connection.actions.spec.ts, auth.credential.spec.ts, SideNavigation.test.tsx, RepositoryUrlForm.test.tsx). Journal step_end for implement-story confirms 'All 8 skips removed, header updated to green phase.' Playbook implement-story prompt (updatedAt: 2026-07-07T19:33:45.906Z) includes 'update any test-file headers that claim tests are skipped, disabled, or in a red-phase state to reflect that the tests are now active, and update story coverage lists in those headers to include the current story.' Ledger entry from 3.12 (2026-07-06T15:31:43.986Z) records the applied update_step.

observations.summary: The 3.9 update_step to implement-story is working — the agent proactively re-read project-context.md and verified all applicable NFR patterns before completing; review-nfrs found 0 findings introduced by Story 4.1 (all 3 findings are pre-existing or infrastructure concerns).
observations.evidence: Journal step_end for implement-story responseExcerpt: 'NFR Pattern Verification (re-reading project-context.md):' table with all patterns checked (strict:true, isolatedModules, noUnusedLocals, tests co-located, ATDD skip removal, no console.log/any/@ts-ignore, Conventional Commits, VERCEL_TOKEN never hardcoded, deploy manual, Nx via yarn prefix). Journal step_end for review-nfrs: '0 findings introduced by Story 4.1; all 3 findings are pre-existing or infrastructure concerns.' nfr-assessment-4-1.md: 'Gate Status: PASS — proceed to release.' Playbook implement-story prompt (updatedAt: 2026-07-07T19:33:45.906Z) includes 'Before marking the story complete, re-read project-context.md and verify each non-functional pattern documented there has been applied to your changes where applicable — apply any missing patterns before finishing, do not wait for the NFR review to catch them.' Ledger entries 5, 37, 43, 52, 59 record prior occurrences (Stories 2.2, 3.8, 3.9, 3.10, 3.11); ledger entries from 3.10, 3.11, 3.12 deduce non-recurrence.

observations.summary: The 3.11 update_step to prepare-tests is working — prepare-tests added an ATDD Artifacts section to the story file with activation instructions, and validate-story-2 found 0 critical issues (only 5 enhancements/optimizations).
observations.evidence: Journal step_end for prepare-tests: 'Story file updated: Added ### ATDD Artifacts section under Dev Notes with activation instructions.' Story file (4-1-provision-the-vercel-project-for-apps-web.md:251-257) contains the ATDD Artifacts section with activation instructions: 'After creating vercel.json (Task 1), remove test.skip() from all tests in vercel-config.spec.ts and run yarn nx test web -- --testPathPattern=vercel-config to verify green phase.' Journal step_end for validate-story-2: '5 improvements applied' (all enhancements/optimizations, 0 critical issues). Playbook prepare-tests prompt (updatedAt: 2026-07-06T12:26:33.858Z) includes 'update the story file's tasks to reflect what was already done — tasks that instruct the dev to create scaffolding that prepare-tests has already applied should be amended to instruct activation of the existing scaffolding instead.' Ledger entries 38, 55 record prior occurrences (Stories 3.8, 3.11); ledger entries from 3.9, 3.10, 3.12 deduce non-recurrence.

observations.summary: The 3.7 update_step to prepare-tests is working — the agent performed a per-AC browser-level mock feasibility check before deferring E2E coverage, documenting the check in the ATDD checklist with a per-AC verdict table.
observations.evidence: Journal step_end for prepare-tests: 'E2E coverage: Deferred — verified no browser-level mock can simulate Vercel API project creation, build pipeline outcomes, or production URL existence. All are server-to-server operations with external side effects. Check recorded in checklist.' ATDD checklist (atdd-checklist-4-1-provision-the-vercel-project-for-apps-web.md:103-116): 'E2E Coverage — Deferred (with browser-level mock check)' section with per-AC feasibility table — AC-1 (Vercel API project creation: 'No mock covers this — defer'), AC-1 (production build: 'No mock covers this — defer'), AC-2 (no GitHub repo: 'No mock covers this — defer'), AC-3 (placeholder URL: 'No mock covers this — defer'). Conclusion: 'No browser-level mock pattern can simulate any of the deferred ACs.' Playbook prepare-tests prompt (updatedAt: 2026-07-06T08:27:13.232Z) includes 'Before deferring E2E coverage, verify no browser-level mock pattern can simulate the scenario — only defer if no mock covers the ACs, and record the check in the ATDD checklist.' Ledger entry 33 (Story 3.7) records original observation; ledger entries from 3.11, 3.12 deduce non-recurrence.
